text.title: Brainstorming Ideation (Agentic Pipeline) Core Rules
text.summary: 5 testable rules grounding brainstorming-ideation. Each rule pairs a binary check + sourced rationale.

rule.statement: Pipeline MUST inject 3-5 distinct personas as generator context. Single-persona generation produces shortlists that collapse to one perspective.
rule.rationale: Persona injection raises cluster count by 40-60% by surfacing perspectives the operator did not consider.

rule.statement: Raw ideas MUST be semantically deduplicated (cosine similarity ≥ 0.85 → merge) before clustering. Naive string-dedup is forbidden.
rule.rationale: LLM generators paraphrase the same idea 3-5x; string-dedup misses near-duplicates and inflates apparent diversity.

rule.statement: Final clustering MUST produce 3-7 themes. Below 3 = under-clustered (no structure); above 7 = over-clustered (no signal).
rule.rationale: 3-7 themes match human working memory and produce actionable categories; outside that band the output becomes noise.

rule.statement: Every shortlisted idea MUST be scored impact (1-5) × effort (1-5). Unscored ideas MUST be flagged 'unscored' explicitly, never silently dropped.
rule.rationale: Without scoring, downstream consumers cannot prioritise; flagging 'unscored' prevents silent loss of candidates.

rule.statement: Every shortlisted idea MUST carry provenance: source persona + technique branch (Classic / Reverse). Anonymous ideas are forbidden in the output.
rule.rationale: Provenance lets the consumer trace why an idea is in the list and which persona to follow up with.
